description: §35.5 — the SPEC's own prescribed remedy for E-LIN-006: declare the `lin` variable INSIDE the same deferred `<request>` body that consumes it. Each scheduled execution of the body then mints and consumes its own fresh linear binding, so dominance is provable within the body and the exactly-once guarantee holds per-execution. The neg twin of lin-006-deferred-ctx-pos: it pins that E-LIN-006 keys on the DECL-vs-CONSUME boundary relationship (the markup-ctx depth at declaration vs at consumption), NOT on the mere presence of a `lin` inside a deferred body — a checker that blanket-rejected `lin` in `<request>` bodies would wrongly fail this. W-PROGRAM-REDUNDANT-LOGIC / W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED co-fire incidentally. No `severity` assertion: severity is asserted on pos twins only (run.ts fails a severity entry for a non-firing code).
rationale: §35.5 scopes E-LIN-006 to a `lin` 'consumed inside a `<request>` or `<poll>` markup body' that 'was declared outside that body', and names the fix: 'Either declare `token` inside the `<request>` body, or pass a consumed (non-lin) value into the body.' Here declaration and consumption are both inside the `<request>` body at the same markup-ctx depth, so no boundary is crossed; `token` is consumed exactly once (§35.3 rule 2) on the single path through the body and no linear diagnostic fires.
